title: Bear Spray Assaults - 100 Block Avenue P South
content:
A 14 year old Saskatoon male is facing charges following an incident where bear spray was deployed into an apartment overnight.
At approximately 2:40 a.m., June 11, 2026, Police were called to the residence in the 100 block of Avenue P South. It was reported that two male youth had kicked the door to the suite down before deploying bear spray and setting off a fire extinguisher. The males fled on foot when officers arrived. One male suspect was brought into custody.
The 14 year old is facing charges of Break and Enter, Assault (x2) and Possession of a Weapon Dangerous to the Public Peace (x2).
The two 34 year old victims suffered the effects of bear spray.
Court information # 991341873.
(Occurrence Number: 26-81852)

title: Stabbing - 3rd Avenue And 23rd Street
content:
Saskatoon Police are investigating a stabbing which took place on board of City of Saskatoon Transit bus yesterday.
Police were called to 3rd Avenue and 23rd Street at approximately 8:30 a.m., Jun 10, 2026. A 29 year old woman reported that she had been in an altercation with another woman on the bus and was stabbed before the suspect fled.
The victim was transported to hospital with non life-threatening injuries. The victim and suspect are known to one another.
Anyone with information is asked to contact the SPS at 306-975-8300 or Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477.
(Occurrence Number: 26-81425)

title: Update - 2nd Arrest - Homicide - 500 Block Avenue X South
content:
A second person has been charged in Saskatoon's fourth homicide this year.
A 21 year old woman was arrested at Pinegrove Correctional Centre on June 10, 2026, in connection with the death of a 39 year old man who was located injured in the 500 block of Avenue X South on April 9, 2026.
The woman is facing charges including; Aggravated Assault, Assault Causing Bodily Harm, Unlawful Confinement, Assault with a Weapon and Uttering Threats.
Court information # 991341837.
The investigation is ongoing.
Release 2026-0348
June 10, 2026 9:09 AM
Members of the Saskatoon Police Service Major Crime Section have arrested a man who is now facing several charges relating to the city’s fourth homicide of 2026.
A 27-year-old man was arrested at the Saskatoon Correctional Centre on June 9, 2026.
He has been charged with manslaughter, unlawful confinement, assault, assault causing bodily harm, assault with a weapon, uttering threats to cause death, aggravated assault and multiple weapons related charges.
He is scheduled to appear in Saskatoon Provincial Court on June 10, 2026.
(Occurrence Number: 26-45704)

title: Bear Spray – 1400 Block Of 20th Street West
content:
Saskatoon Police are investigating in relation to a bear spray incident that occurred yesterday.
On June 9, 2026 at approximately 5:40 p.m., police were called to the 1400 block of 20th Street West for a report of an injured person. It was reported to police that someone had discharged bear spray.
Officers arrived on scene and located multiple people suffering from the effects of bear spray. The suspect had fled the scene prior to police arrival. It is believed that the suspect and victims are known to each other.
Anyone with information that may assist in this investigation is asked to contact police at 306-975-8300 or submit an anonymous tip to Crime Stoppers at 1-800-222-8477.
(Occurrence Number: 26-81164)

title: Arrests - Robbery - 800 Block Of 29th Street West
content:
Three people were arrested and face charges relating to a robbery involving a weapon early Tuesday afternoon.
At approximately 2:20 p.m. on June 9, 2026, police were dispatched to a report of a robbery in progress at a business in the 800 block of 29th Street West. Call information outlined that a female threatened staff with bear spray and demanded cash before fleeing the scene with multiple other people.
Responding officers located three people matching suspect descriptions in the 300 block of Avenue I North where they were arrested without further incident. A search incident to arrest resulted in police locating two cans of bear spray, a knife and an undisclosed amount of cash.
As a result, a 24-year-old woman has been charged with robbery, possession of a weapon and breaching court-imposed conditions. A 25-year-old woman has been charged with robbery, possession of a weapon (x2), and carrying a concealed weapon. A 22-year-old man has been charged with possession of a weapon and carrying a concealed weapon.
(Court Information Number(s): 991341726, 991341727, 991341728)
(Occurrence Number: 26-80945)

title: Charges Laid – Robbery – 200 Block Of 1st Avenue South
content:
One man has been arrested and is facing charges in relation to a robbery at a business on Tuesday afternoon.
On June 9, 2026 at around 4:40 p.m., police received a report of a robbery in the 200 block of 1st Avenue South. It was reported that a male stole from a business and threatened security with a hatchet before fleeing on foot.
Upon police arrival, officers learned security staff had detained the suspect and he was subsequently arrested without further incident.
The 23-year-old man has been charged with theft under $5000, possession of a weapon dangerous to public peace, carrying a concealed weapon, mischief under $5000, assaulting a peace officer, assault with a weapon (x2), and breaching court-imposed conditions.
(Court Information Number: 991341734)
(Occurrence Number: 26-81135)

title: UPDATE: Charges Laid - Homicide - 500 Block Avenue X South
content:
UPDATE: Release 2026-0352
June 11, 2026 9:18 AM
A second person has been charged in Saskatoon's fourth homicide this year.
A 21 year old woman was arrested at Pinegrove Correctional Centre on June 10, 2026, in connection with the death of a 39 year old man who was located injured in the 500 block of Avenue X South on April 9, 2026.
The woman is facing charges including; Aggravated Assault, Assault Causing Bodily Harm, Unlawful Confinement, Assault with a Weapon and Uttering Threats.
Court information # 991341837.
The investigation is ongoing.
Members of the Saskatoon Police Service Major Crime Section have arrested a man who is now facing several charges relating to the city’s fourth homicide of 2026.
A 27-year-old man was arrested at the Saskatoon Correctional Centre on June 9, 2026.
He has been charged with manslaughter, unlawful confinement, assault, assault causing bodily harm, assault with a weapon, uttering threats to cause death, aggravated assault and multiple weapons related charges.
He is scheduled to appear in Saskatoon Provincial Court on June 10, 2026.
Release 2026-0210
April 9, 2026 12:01 PM
The Major Crime Section is investigating following the city’s fourth homicide.
At around 3:45 a.m. on April 9, 2026, members of Patrol responded to the 500 block of Avenue X South for a report of an injured person. Upon arrival, officers located a 39-year-old unresponsive male. He was transported to hospital by Medavie Health Services West where he was later pronounced deceased.
Members of the Major Crime and Forensic Identification Sections are continuing to investigate.
(Occurrence Number: 26-45704)

title: Arrest - Saskatchewan Internet Child Exploitation Unit Investigation - Spruce Home
content:
The Saskatchewan Internet Child Exploitation (ICE) Unit has charged a Spruce Home man with child exploitation related offences.
On June 8, 2026, the Saskatchewan ICE Unit executed a Search Warrant at a home in the hamlet of Spruce Home. Electronic devices were examined at scene and also seized by investigators.
As a result of the investigation, 33-year-old Christopher John Goreham of was arrested and charged on June 8, 2026 with the following:
Two counts of Possessing Child Sexual Abuse and Exploitation Material
One count of Making Available Child Sexual Abuse and Exploitation Material
One count of Accessing Child Sexual Abuse and Exploitation Material
Goreham appeared in court on June 9, 2026 and was released with court-imposed conditions. His next court date is scheduled for June 25, 2026 at 1:30 pm in Prince Albert Provincial Court.
(Court information number: 90514672)
The Saskatchewan ICE Unit is comprised of investigators from the Saskatchewan RCMP, Regina Police Service and Saskatoon Police Service. Their mandate is to investigate crimes involving Child Sexual Abuse and Exploitation Material.
For media inquiries, please contact: S/Sgt. Tim Failler, Saskatoon Police Service, 306-986-6164.

title: Charges Laid - Saskatchewan RCMP / Corman Park Police Service Assist - Evade Police
content:
Members of the Saskatoon Police Service arrested a man who fled from a Corman Park Police Service officer early Friday morning.
On June 5, 2026 at approximately 1:20 a.m., Saskatoon Police received a request for assistance from the Saskatchewan RCMP regarding an incident near Martensville where a vehicle had evaded a Corman Park Police Service member during a traffic stop. The suspect vehicle reportedly fled southbound towards Saskatoon.
The Saskatoon Police Service Air Support Unit (ASU) began monitoring from above and was able to locate the vehicle travelling into Saskatoon at a high rate of speed. The vehicle continued driving in this manner for nearly an hour before coming to a stop in the 100 block of Avenue P South.
Throughout the occurrence, ASU observed the vehicle stop on numerous occasions and drop off passengers.
The lone remaining occupant of the vehicle, the driver, was arrested without further incident. A search of his person incident to arrest resulted in officers locating suspected methamphetamine. A search of the vehicle resulted on officers locating a baton.
As a result, the 31-year-old man has been charged with dangerous driving, possession of a weapon, possession of methamphetamine and breaching court-imposed conditions.
(Court Information Number: 991341283)
(Occurrence Number: 26-78511)

title: Arrest - Assault P.O. - 800 Block Of Confederation Drive
content:
One male youth has been charged with two counts of assaulting a peace officer during an arrest relating to a mischief incident Saturday night.
On June 6, 2026 at approximately 9:25 p.m., officers arrested a male in the 800 block of Confederation Drive who matched the description of a suspect in a break and enter and mischief incident moments prior where It was reported that a male was climbing up a balcony.
Upon arrest, the male began to resist and while officers attempted to place him in the patrol car, he kicked a police officer. A further search of his person incident to arrest resulted in police locating a cannister of bear spray.
Officers also attended the scene of the mischief incident where several flower pots were observed to have been broken and pushed off a balcony.
As a result of this incident, the 17-year-old male youth has been charged with assaulting a peace officer (x2), possession of a weapon and carrying a concealed weapon.
(Court Information Number: 991341429)
(Occurrence Number: 26-79672)
